reason: The text explicitly names and gives dialogue to two regular people who are not elite: Shiphrah (שִׁפְרָה) and Puah (פּוּעָה), who are Hebrew midwives. They are identified by name and are given direct speech in which they address Pharaoh and explain their actions (וַתֹּאמַרְןָ הַֽמְיַלְּדֹת֙ אֶל־ פַּרְעֹה). As midwives, they represent the working class, not the political or social elite, yet they are both named and given narrative voice. This directly contradicts the statement's claim that only the elite are named or have lines.
